description_long: This is a roughly hewn, irregularly shaped stone with a predominantly white coloration and subtle gray-green veining throughout its surface. The stone has a jagged, angular profile with one side showing a more pronounced, sloped peak, while the opposite side appears flatter and broader. Its texture is visibly coarse and unpolished, with small chips and natural fractures along the edges, suggesting it has not been artificially shaped or smoothed. The stone's surface displays a matte finish, with some areas showing slight discoloration or mineral inclusions, adding to its natural appearance. The base of the stone is relatively flat, allowing it to rest stably on the ground, while the upper surfaces are uneven and rugged. This type of stone is commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or as part of landscaping features, serving as a natural obstacle or decorative element.
description: A jagged, white stone with gray-green veining and a rough, unpolished surface. Its irregular shape and coarse texture make it suitable as a natural barrier or decorative feature in urban environments.
description_view_0: The stone appears broad and squat, with a gently sloping peak and visible gray-green veining on a mostly white surface.
description_view_1: This view highlights the stone's angular side, revealing a rough, brownish edge and a more pronounced slope.
description_view_2: The stone's peak is more prominent from this angle, with the surface showing subtle veining and a rugged, uneven texture.
description_view_3: A flatter, wider face is visible, with scattered gray-green patches and a generally matte, coarse finish.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on the ground
attribute_pattern: Subtle, irregular gray-green veining and mottling across a predominantly white surface, with no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and unpolished, with minor chips and natural fractures indicating some exposure to the elements but not extensive aging.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts moderately with manicured lawns or paved surfaces, but can blend with other natural stones or vegetation in landscaped areas.
